item.title: Italy Rising Power Costs Reveal Gas Reliance That’s Hard to Diversify
item.description: The war in Iran has forced Italy to reckon with a dependence on gas that’s exposed the country to bigger energy spikes than its peers and put hard-won economic progress at risk.

item.title: Russian Steel Use Drops 15% in First Quarter as Economy Slows
item.description: A decline in Russian steel consumption accelerated in the first quarter as the economy slowed, according to Severstal PJSC, one of the country’s top steelmakers.

item.title: The Music Executive Orchestrating Ye’s Risky Comeback Tour
item.description: Larry Jackson has shown a knack for handling idiosyncratic artists.

item.title: Blue Energy Raises $380 Million to Build Nukes for Data Centers
item.description: The startup Blue Energy Global Inc. raised $380 million to develop small, prefabricated nuclear reactors to run data centers.

item.title: Jefferies Picks Tanks and Ammo Over Air Defense After War Slump
item.description: Shares in European armored vehicle and ammunition makers look attractive again after falling sharply since the start of the Iran war as investors prioritized air defense, according to analysts at Jefferies.

item.title: Narrow US Stocks Rally Shows Investors Are Cautious
item.description: Low volume, narrow leadership show some traders are unconvinced

item.title: CIC, NBIM, BlackRock Said to Invest in Victory Giant Listing
item.description: Victory Giant Technology Huizhou Co. has attracted Chinese and global investors including China Investment Corp. and Norges Bank Investment Management, the world’s largest sovereign fund, to its $2.6 billion share sale, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Pakistan Receives $1 Billion Saudi Boost as UAE Seeks Repayment
item.description: Pakistan has received the final $1 billion tranche of a $3 billion support package from Saudi Arabia, shoring up the South Asian nation’s foreign exchange reserves after the UAE declined to rollover its debt.

item.title: Emerging-Market Assets Eye Record Close as Iran Talks Loom
item.description: Emerging-market stocks climbed toward a record high, lifted by technology shares, as investors weighed whether Iran will join talks that could extend a Middle East truce and help restore energy flows.

item.title: UN Arm Mulls $100 Million Bond for Kenyan Biodiversity Projects
item.description: The Biodiversity Finance Initiative is holding early-stage talks on issuing a $100 million bond to fund marine and terrestrial projects on the Kenyan coast.

item.title: South African Business Optimism Wanes in March on Iran Conflict
item.description: South African business confidence moderated in March as the US-Israeli war with Iran roiled financial, commodity and foreign-exchange markets.

item.title: Engie Is in Talks With US to Relinquish Offshore Wind Leases
item.description: Engie SA is in talks with President Donald Trump’s administration to relinquish US leases for offshore wind projects that he opposes, said Chief Executive Officer Catherine MacGregor.

item.title: Billionaire Potanin Must Face $5 Billion Divorce Case in London
item.description: Sanctioned Russian billionaire Vladimir Potanin suffered a legal setback at a London court after a judge threw out an attempt to delay his ex-wife from getting a final hearing in her $5 billion divorce spat.

item.title: JPMorgan Lifts S&P 500 Target as AI Rally Regains Momentum
item.description: A renewed surge in enthusiasm for artificial intelligence will help drive US stocks to higher-than-expected levels this year, according to strategists at JPMorgan Chase & Co.

item.title: BOJ Is Said Leaning to April Hold While Keeping Hawkish Stance
item.description: The Bank of Japan is leaning toward keeping its policy rate unchanged next week given uncertainties stemming from the war in Iran, with officials still committed to raising borrowing costs sooner or later, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Digital Finance Providers Seek EU Carve-Out From New Legislation
item.description: Digital finance providers want a carve-out from forthcoming European legislation governing distributed ledger technology, arguing that Europe is losing out to the US.

item.title: Russia’s Grip on Bulgaria Will Test New Leader’s Anti-Graft Push
item.description: Bulgaria’s political and economic ties to Russia pose the biggest risk to Prime Minister-elect Rumen Radev’s plan to fight corruption.

item.title: German Investor Outlook Drops to Worst Since 2022 on Iran War
item.description: German investor optimism dropped to the lowest level in more than three years as the Iran war eats away at a rebound in Europe’s biggest economy.

item.title: Taiwan’s Export Orders Surge the Most in 16 Years on AI Demand
item.description: Taiwan saw its export orders surge at the fastest pace in 16 years, a sign that demand for its AI-related products remains robust despite disruptions to global trade from tensions in the Middle East.

item.title: Barclays Faces Scrutiny From UK Regulators Over Its SRT Deals
item.description: UK regulators have told Barclays Plc to assess its processes for significant risk transfers, transactions that allow banks to offload loan risk and bolster their capacity to grow.

item.title: TikTok Makes Americans Want Chinese EVs They Can’t Have
item.description: Chinese brands are winning over American consumers on social media, undermining US efforts to keep them out.

item.title: Gunvor CEO Pedersen Says Törnqvist Loan Isn’t Barrier To Growth
item.description: A multibillion-dollar loan from former owner Torbjörn Törnqvist won’t constrain Gunvor Group’s growth, its new chief executive officer said.

item.title: Euro Attracts Bullish Options Buyers as Currency Goes Off Script
item.description: The euro is the second-best G-10 performer against the dollar over the past month, defying expectations that the energy shock caused by the Middle East war would hit Europe’s economy and drag the currency lower.

item.title: Guindos Says ECB Needs to Be Prudent, Keep a Cool Head on Rates
item.description: Uncertainty over the Iran war means the European Central Bank should take a measured approach to setting interest rates, according to Vice President Luis de Guindos.

item.title: Indian Wind Turbine Giant Says Nation Set to Meet 2030 Target
item.description: Suzlon Energy Ltd., India’s largest wind turbine maker, said the nation is on course to hit its goal of 100 gigawatts of wind capacity by the end of the decade, as installations accelerate to meet growing demand for round-the-clock supply.

item.title: Oil Prices Don’t Reflect Scale of Supply Hit, Analysts Say
item.description: Oil prices don’t fully reflect the largest supply disruption ever, after the Iran war effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz, some of the market’s top analysts said.

item.title: BOJ Flags Risk to Japan Bonds From Foreign Hedge Funds Unwinding
item.description: The Bank of Japan cautioned that a potential unwinding of positions by hedge funds globally may spread to Japan’s bond market.

item.title: Middle East Conflict Clouds Thai Banks Outlook as Profits Slip
item.description: Two major Thai banks struck a cautious tone on their 2026 outlook after reporting weak first-quarter net income, underscoring growing risks from Middle East conflict and a global energy shock.

item.title: Carlsberg Dumps Coca-Cola, Bets on Pepsi in Nordic Markets
item.description: Carlsberg A/S is preparing a major shift in its soft drinks strategy, replacing Coke with Pepsi across much of Northern Europe in a move set to reshape the region’s beverage market.

item.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook’s 15-Year Legacy by the Numbers
item.description: Fifteen years after succeeding Steve Jobs, Tim Cook is handing over the top leadership position at Apple Inc. to hardware specialist John Ternus. The outgoing chief executive officer, who’ll stay on as executive chairman, built up an unprecedented record of success over his tenure. Here are some of the numerical highlights.
